gaps.description: All 15 harness scripts exist as design specifications only. Zero lines of actual bash code written. Zero production runs.
gaps.what_we_know: Full specifications in SCRIPT_REGISTRY.json with input/output/dependencies documented
gaps.what_we_need: Actual bash implementations, tested on Windows (Git Bash) and Linux
gaps.resolution_path: Build scripts in ~/.claude/scripts/ following specifications. Test each individually, then integration test the full loop.

gaps.description: The evaluator agent needs to be independently tuned for scepticism. Anthropic needed several rounds of calibration. We haven't started.
gaps.what_we_know: Evaluator should default to fail if in doubt. Must use tools (Playwright) not just read code. Calibration gate plants deliberate failure.
gaps.what_we_need: Iterative tuning of evaluator prompt across multiple real sprints
gaps.resolution_path: Run evaluator on known-good and known-bad codebases. Adjust scepticism level until calibration gate consistently catches planted failures.

gaps.description: All harness scripts assume bash (Git Bash on Windows). Need to verify: jq availability, process management (kill, PID files), lockfiles, curl, git. Windows path handling (forward slashes).
gaps.what_we_know: User is on Windows 10, using Git Bash via Claude Code
gaps.what_we_need: Test each script on Windows Git Bash. Identify Windows-specific gotchas.
gaps.resolution_path: Build and test scripts on the actual target platform (Windows Git Bash). Install jq if missing.

gaps.description: Hook registration in settings.json — exact syntax for matcher patterns, tool name filtering, PostToolUse vs Stop hooks. Need to verify against current Claude Code version.
gaps.what_we_know: PostToolUse hooks exist. Can filter by tool_name. Stop hooks fire at session end.
gaps.what_we_need: Exact settings.json format validated against Claude Code docs
gaps.resolution_path: Read Claude Code documentation. Test hook registration with a simple echo script.

gaps.description: How the generator and evaluator actually negotiate in separate sessions. Does the evaluator get a fresh session? How is the feedback loop structured?
gaps.what_we_know: Max 3 negotiation attempts. Generator proposes, evaluator approves or rejects with feedback.
gaps.what_we_need: Real-world testing of the NEGOTIATE phase across multiple iterations
gaps.resolution_path: Test on a real feature. Observe negotiation dynamics. Adjust prompts.

gaps.description: Architecture designed for single-model (Opus) first. Multi-model is future work — Opus for Plan/Review, Sonnet for Execute, etc.
gaps.what_we_know: File-driven state machine is model-agnostic. Model profiles documented for 4+ models.
gaps.what_we_need: Testing with Sonnet as generator, MiniMax for summarisation, Codex for browser tasks
gaps.resolution_path: After single-model harness is stable, test model substitution at each phase.

gaps.description: Budget circuit breaker uses iteration count * ~$4 as cost proxy. Actual costs vary significantly by session length and model.
gaps.what_we_know: Claude Code doesn't expose token counts in bash. Iteration count is rough proxy.
gaps.what_we_need: Better cost estimation or API usage tracking integration
blocking: Nothing — current proxy is acceptable for safety
gaps.resolution_path: Monitor actual costs against estimates over several harness runs. Adjust multiplier.

gaps.description: The Telegram polling daemon, notification script, and WhatsApp alternative are designed but not built. This is optional — the harness works without it.
gaps.what_we_know: Telegram Bot API via BotFather, curl wrapper for sendMessage, polling for getUpdates. WhatsApp via Twilio as alternative. Transport is interchangeable.
gaps.what_we_need: Actual bot token, chat ID, and working scripts tested against Telegram API
blocking: Nothing — harness works without notification. Human can check state files manually.
gaps.resolution_path: Create Telegram bot via BotFather, get token and chat ID, implement notify.sh and telegram-poll.sh, test end-to-end.
